title.en: The Benevolent Beast That Treads on No Living Thing

summary.zh: 麋身、牛尾、独角的仁兽，行不践生草、不伤生灵，唯圣王在位时现身；孔子闻"西狩获麟"而绝笔《春秋》。
summary.en: A one-horned beast with a deer's body and ox's tail that steps on no living grass and appears only under sage rulers; word that a lin had been caught made Confucius lay down his brush.

description.zh:
麒麟位列"四灵"，雄曰麒，雌曰麟。《说文解字》释"麒"为仁兽，"麋身牛尾一角"；旧注补足细节：独角之端有肉，示其有武而不用。传说它行走时不践生草、不踩活虫，不入陷阱、不罹罗网，音中钟吕、行中规矩，唯有圣王在位、天下太平之时才肯现身，因此是最高等级的祥瑞。

最著名的记载在《春秋》的末尾：鲁哀公十四年春，"西狩获麟"。《公羊传》说孔子听闻猎获的是麟，反袂拭面，涕泪沾袍，叹道"吾道穷矣"——仁兽出非其时而被捕获，孔子于是搁笔，《春秋》至此而终，"获麟绝笔"成为后世典故。《礼记·礼运》将"麟、凤、龟、龙"并称四灵，麟居其首。

民间信仰里麒麟主送子，宋以后"麒麟送子"年画盛行，童子骑麟自天而降；杰出子弟被赞为"麒麟儿"。明永乐十二年（1414年），榜葛剌国（今孟加拉一带）使团向明成祖进献长颈鹿，朝野以为麒麟降世，沈度所绘《瑞应麒麟图》存世至今；日语至今仍称长颈鹿为"麒麟"。
description.en:
The qilin heads the Four Numinous Beasts; qi names the male and lin the female. The Shuowen Jiezi defines it as the 'benevolent beast', with the body of a deer, the tail of an ox, and a single horn — a horn tipped with flesh, the commentators add, a weapon carried but never used. It walks without crushing a blade of living grass or a single insect, falls into no pit and springs no snare, its call matching the pitch-pipes and its gait the compass and square. It shows itself only when a sage rules and the world is at peace, which made it the highest omen of all.

Its most famous appearance closes the Spring and Autumn Annals: in the spring of the fourteenth year of Duke Ai, 'in a hunt to the west, a lin was taken'. The Gongyang commentary describes Confucius wiping his face with his sleeve, tears soaking his robe: 'My way has run its course.' The benevolent beast had come in the wrong age and been captured, and the master laid down his brush; 'the capture of the lin' has meant a great work's final line ever since. The Book of Rites lists lin, phoenix, tortoise, and dragon together as the Four Numinous Beasts.

Popular religion made the qilin a bringer of sons: New Year prints from the Song onward show an infant riding one down from heaven, and gifted children are still praised as 'qilin boys'. When a Bengali embassy presented a giraffe to the Yongle emperor in 1414, court and country hailed it as a qilin — Shen Du's commemorative painting survives — and Japanese still uses 'kirin' as the word for giraffe.

value.zh: 麋身、牛尾、独角，角端有肉
value.en: Deer's body, ox's tail, one flesh-tipped horn
Treads on no living grass, harms no creature
The lin captured in the west; Confucius lays down his brush

sources.en: Spring and Autumn Annals, 14th year of Duke Ai, with the Gongyang commentary
